message:
harden(merkle,byte_compression): bounds-check usize→u32 length prefixes

Multi-pass bug-hunt — fourth-instance sweep for the same class as
recording.rs c3b59e4a (WAR frame payload_len silent truncation).
wal_engine.rs already has the right pattern (explicit u32::MAX check
before the cast); these two were holdouts.

merkle_tree.rs::leaf
  Hash a key-value leaf node prefixed by `(key.len() as u32, value.len()
  as u32)`. If a caller ever produced a key OR value at or above
  u32::MAX bytes, the length prefix would silently wrap and two distinct
  (key, value) pairs whose lengths collide modulo 2^32 would hash to
  the same leaf — defeating the length-prefix domain separation that
  the design relies on. Real merkle leaves are KV-pair-sized so this is
  defense in depth, but the failure mode (silent hash collision) is
  catastrophic enough to warrant an explicit `assert!` in both branches
  rather than letting truncation slip through.

byte_compression.rs::compress_batch
  Layout `[count: u32-le] [len0: u32-le] [data0] [len1: u32-le] ...`.
  Both `count = inputs.len() as u32` and `compressed.len() as u32`
  silently truncate. Replace with saturating `u32::try_from`, iterate
  only up to the stored count, and `debug_assert!` on the per-buffer
  length so dev/test catches the case while release falls back to a
  stream that's internally consistent (decompress_batch's bounds checks
  surface InvalidInput rather than producing garbage).
